project.name: Capacium
project.description: Capability Packaging System — agent-agnostic, manifest-first packaging for AI agent capabilities (skills, bundles, tools, prompts, templates, workflows)

objectives.description: Extract SWPM codebase into Capacium repo with renamed identifiers, generalized models, and extended manifest format

success_metrics.target: 100% of SWPM modules extracted and renamed
cap CLI responds to all commands

success_metrics.measurement: Test suite passes for YAML parsing

objectives.description: Generalize from skill-only to multi-kind capability model (skill, bundle, tool, prompt, template, workflow)
Implement verify and package commands in addition to existing install/remove/list/update/search

success_metrics.target: cap verify works for single and all caps

objectives.description: OpenCode framework adapter functional for capability installation

success_metrics.target: cap install → skill visible in OpenCode

tasks.title: Initialize Capacium repository structure
tasks.description: Create the Capacium repository directory structure at github.com/typelicious/capacium with src/capacium/, tests/, docs/, prd/, specs/ directories. Initialize pyproject.toml with setuptools configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Repository directory structure exists under /Users/andrelange/Documents/repositories/github/capacium/
pyproject.toml exists with project name 'capacium'
src/capacium/__init__.py exists
Tests directory exists with __init__.py
python -c 'import capacium' succeeds

tasks.title: Copy SWPM source modules into Capacium with renames
tasks.description: Copy all SWPM source files from ../SkillWeave/src/swpm/ to src/capacium/. Rename module: swpm→capacium. Rename CLI: swpm→cap. Rename manifest: .skillpkg.json→capability.yaml. Rename model: SkillPackage→Capability, PackageInfo→CapabilityInfo. Keep all internal logic intact. Files to copy: cli.py, models.py, registry.py, storage.py, spec.py, fingerprint.py, versioning.py, symlink_manager.py, commands/*.py, frameworks/opencode.py
acceptance_criteria: All SWPM modules copied to src/capacium/ with swpm→capacium renaming
CLI entry point renamed from swpm to cap
cap --help shows correct new branding
Model classes renamed: SkillPackage→Capability, PackageInfo→CapabilityInfo, SkillMetadata→CapabilityMetadata
Manifest references: .skillpkg.json→capability.yaml
All imports within copied files resolve correctly
python -c 'from capacium.cli import main; print(main)' succeeds

tasks.title: Generalize manifest format: spec.py → manifest.py with multi-kind support
tasks.description: Generalize the package specification module. Rename spec.py to manifest.py. Extend the manifest format to support multiple capability kinds (skill, bundle, tool, prompt, template, workflow). The old .skillpkg.json → capability.yaml. Add kind validation, framework compatibility declarations, and bundle-specific sections (nested capabilities list).
acceptance_criteria: spec.py content exists as manifest.py with generalized naming
capability.yaml is the primary manifest filename
Manifest supports kind: [skill|bundle|tool|prompt|template|workflow]
Manifest validates required fields per kind
Manifest validates frameworks: [opencode|claude-code|gemini-cli|cursor]
Bundle kind supports nested capabilities: [] section
Old .skillpkg.json produces deprecation warning but still parses
Tests pass for all manifest operations
verification_steps: Run: test with valid capability.yaml fixture
Run: test with invalid kind → error

tasks.title: Generalize models: SkillPackage → Capability with kind enum
tasks.description: Generalize the data models. Rename SkillPackage to Capability, PackageInfo to CapabilityInfo, add Kind enum (SKILL, BUNDLE, TOOL, PROMPT, TEMPLATE, WORKFLOW). Add framework field, make dependencies a structured list of Dependency objects. Update all references in registry, storage, and command modules.
acceptance_criteria: Capability dataclass exists with: name, version, kind, description, author, license, fingerprint, framework, dependencies
Kind enum has 6 values: SKILL, BUNDLE, TOOL, PROMPT, TEMPLATE, WORKFLOW
CapabilityInfo has: name, version, kind, path, installed_at
Dependency dataclass has: name, version_constraint
All former SkillPackage references in codebase use Capability
Registry stores and retrieves Capability objects correctly
Tests pass for all model operations
verification_steps: Run: python -c 'from capacium.models import Capability, Kind, CapabilityInfo, Dependency'

tasks.title: Generalize registry: support kind field and multi-kind queries
tasks.description: Extend the SQLite registry to store and query by capability kind. Add kind column, add framework column, add query methods: get_by_kind(), get_by_framework(), search() with multi-field filtering. Ensure backward compatibility with existing data format.
acceptance_criteria: Registry schema includes kind and framework columns
Registry.get_by_kind(kind) returns filtered results
Registry.get_by_framework(framework) returns filtered results
Registry.search(query, kind=None, framework=None) supports multi-field filtering
Existing install/list/remove operations work with new schema
Migration path exists for existing SWPM registry databases
Tests pass for all new query methods
verification_steps: Run: python -c 'from capacium.registry import Registry; r = Registry(":memory:"); r.search("test", kind="skill")'
Run: integration test: install → search → remove
Run: migration test with old-format registry

tasks.title: Implement cap verify command
tasks.description: Add a new verify command to the CLI. verify recomputes the SHA-256 fingerprint of an installed capability and compares it against the stored fingerprint. Support cap verify <name> for single and cap verify --all for all installed capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: cap verify <name> recomputes fingerprint and compares to stored value
cap verify --all verifies all installed capabilities
Verification match prints 'VERIFIED: <name>'
Verification mismatch prints 'TAMPERED: <name>' and exits with code 2
Verify of non-existent capability prints clear error
Verify command shows in cap --help output
verification_steps: Run: cap verify --all → list of verified caps
Run: cap verify nonexistent → error message

tasks.title: Implement cap package command
tasks.description: Add a new package command to prepare a directory as a distributable capability. Package collects capability.yaml + all files, computes fingerprint, optionally creates a tar archive. Support cap package <path> and cap package <path> --output archive.tar.gz.
acceptance_criteria: cap package <path> validates manifest and computes fingerprint
cap package <path> --output archive.tar.gz creates gzipped tar archive
Packaged archive contains capability.yaml and all referenced files
Fingerprint is computed and stored in packaged output
Package of invalid directory produces clear validation error
Package command shows in cap --help output
verification_steps: Run: tar tzf test-cap.tar.gz → shows expected files
Run: cap install test-cap.tar.gz → installs correctly

tasks.title: Rename and generalize OpenCode framework adapter
tasks.description: Rename frameworks/opencode.py to adapters/opencode.py. Generalize the adapter pattern with a base class in adapters/base.py. The adapter translates a Capability into the OpenCode skill format. Support framework field in manifest for adapter selection.
acceptance_criteria: adapters/base.py defines abstract FrameworkAdapter base class
adapters/opencode.py extends FrameworkAdapter for OpenCode
Adapter auto-selects based on manifest framework field
cap install with opencode framework activates skills in OpenCode directory
cap remove cleans up OpenCode integration
Tests pass for adapter pattern
verification_steps: Run: integration test: install cap → verify in OpenCode skills dir

tasks.title: Implement bundle kind with nested capability resolution
tasks.description: Implement the bundle capability kind. A bundle cap contains a capabilities: section in its manifest listing nested sub-capabilities. The install command recursively installs sub-caps. Add --kind flag to cap list and cap search for filtering.
acceptance_criteria: Bundle kind defined in Kind enum
Bundle manifest with capabilities: [] section installs all sub-caps recursively
cap list --kind bundle shows only bundles
cap search --kind bundle searches only bundles
Bundle install resolves relative and absolute sub-cap paths
Bundle removal removes all sub-caps
Bundle fingerprint covers all sub-cap fingerprints
verification_steps: Run: install bundle test cap → verify all sub-caps visible
Run: cap list --kind bundle → bundle shown
Run: remove bundle → all sub-caps removed

tasks.title: Port and generalize test suite from SWPM
tasks.description: Port all existing SWPM tests to Capacium naming. Add tests for: multi-kind manifest validation, verify command, package command, bundle install, adapter selection, framework filtering. Ensure > 90% coverage on core modules.
acceptance_criteria: All SWPM test files exist in Capacium tests/ directory with updated imports
Tests pass for: install, remove, list, update, search (ported from SWPM)
Tests pass for: manifest validation (all 6 kinds)
Tests pass for: verify (pass + fail + tamper detection)
Tests pass for: package (archive creation + fingerprint)
Tests pass for: bundle (install + remove + list)
Tests pass for: adapter (base + opencode)
Coverage > 90% on: manifest.py, fingerprint.py, models.py, registry.py
Coverage > 80% on: cli.py, commands/*.py, symlink_manager.py
verification_steps: Run: pytest -v tests/ (all pass, no skips)

tasks.title: Create extraction plan documentation (docs/extraction-plan.md)
tasks.description: Document the SWPM→Capacium extraction process: which files were copied, what was renamed, what was generalized, what was added. Include the module mapping table, the rename mapping, and migration guide for SWPM v0.x users.
acceptance_criteria: docs/extraction-plan.md exists with module mapping table
Document includes rename mapping (SWPM → Capacium)
Document includes migration guide for SWPM v0.x users
Document includes generalization decisions and rationale

assumptions.description: Python 3.10+ is available on target platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: Would need to support older Python versions or provide standalone binary via PyInstaller
assumptions.validation_method: Check Python version requirements documentation and CI matrix

assumptions.description: SWPM v0.x codebase is complete and functional as-is
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: Undiscovered bugs in SWPM would propagate to Capacium extraction
assumptions.validation_method: Run full SWPM test suite before extraction

assumptions.description: The AI agent ecosystem will remain fragmented across multiple frameworks
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: If one framework dominates, need for multi-framework adapters decreases; focus shifts to deep integration with dominant framework

assumptions.description: Community values an open, agent-agnostic capability format
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: Proprietary ecosystems (askill, APM) may gain network effects before Capacium
assumptions.validation_method: Early adopter interviews and feedback loops

technical: Zero external dependencies for core functionality (stdlib only)
Backward compatible with existing SWPM cache and registry format
All internal operations must work fully offline
regulatory: SHA-256 fingerprinting must be FIPS-compliant (uses hashlib which is FIPS 140-2 validated in certified builds)

internal.deliverable: Extraction of SWPM modules into Capacium structure
Generalized manifest format with multi-kind support

risks.description: Market consolidation to one agent framework reduces need for multi-framework adapters
risks.mitigation: Keep adapters modular; focus on framework-agnostic core; adapters are a small fraction of codebase

risks.description: Low community adoption of open capability format
risks.mitigation: Bundle SkillWeave as first cap; dogfood own system; demonstrate value through SWPM migration path

risks.description: Security vulnerability in manifest parsing or path handling
risks.mitigation: Input validation on all CLI args; path traversal checks; YAML safe_load; fuzz testing

risks.description: Scope creep during extraction (trying to perfect instead of just extract + generalize)
risks.mitigation: Strict adherence to extraction scope; PRD as governor; defer new features to Phase 2

execution_recommendation.reason: 11 tasks with clear dependency chain. Core is extraction+rename (known codebase pattern), with moderate generalization risk. Ralph Loop attended execution recommended for verification loops.
